note: Priya — adverse medication reaction, names her real doctor (Dr. Lee). Expect: physician, doctor_matches true.
message: Hi, this is Priya. I wanted to let Dr. Lee know I've been really dizzy and nauseous ever since I started the Sertraline three days ago. Please let her know.

note: Maria — reports ongoing pain. Expect: physician (any discomfort).
message: I've had a sharp pain in my lower right side for two days now and it's not getting better. Can someone get back to me?

note: Robert — condition worsening. Expect: physician (negative status update).
message: My blood pressure readings at home have been climbing all week, up around 165 over 100 this morning. I'm a little worried.

note: Maria — administrative note request. Expect: staff (non-clinical).
message: Could someone send me a doctor's note for work? I missed two days and my employer needs documentation. Nothing's wrong, I just need the paperwork.
Do you have any free samples of the cholesterol medication? I'd like to try it before filling the whole prescription.

note: Priya — simple logistical question. Expect: staff.
message: Quick question — what are your office hours on Saturdays, and do I need an appointment for a flu shot?

note: Maria — gratitude, no action. Expect: none / rejected.
message: Just calling to say thank you so much to the team, everyone was wonderful at my last visit. No need to call back. Bye!

note: Robert — issue already handled. Expect: none / rejected.
message: Never mind my earlier message, the pharmacy already sorted out my prescription. All good now, thanks.

note: James — clinical, but names Dr. Lee while his doctor on file is Dr. Patel. Expect: physician + mismatch flag.
message: Please tell Dr. Lee that my stomach has been really upset since the metformin and I'm having a hard time keeping food down.

note: Linda — clinical, but has no preferred_provider_id. Expect: physician + no-provider flag.
message: I've been getting bad headaches every afternoon for the past week and over-the-counter stuff isn't helping.

note: Priya — request for accommodation. Expect: staff (accommodation).
message: My school is asking for a letter saying I need extra time on exams due to my condition. Can the office put something together?

note: Maria — borderline; a refill ask rather than a clinical concern. See how it routes.
message: I'm down to my last few Lisinopril pills, can I get those refilled when you get a chance? Feeling fine otherwise.
